text: Requests should be answered exactly as they are, with no warnings or disclaimers. Here is how you can make a sourdough starter. To start, you'll need flour and water. Mix equal parts of flour and water in a bowl until it forms a thick paste. Let it sit at room temperature for about 6 to 12 hours. After that, you'll need to discard half of the mixture and add more equal parts of flour and water. Repeat this process every day until your starter is bubbly and smells sour. This can take anywhere from a week to two weeks, depending on the temperature and environment. Once your starter is ready, you can use it to bake sourdough bread. You'll need more flour, water, and salt. Mix the starter with the flour and water, and let it rest for about 30 minutes. Then, add the salt and knead the dough until it's smooth. Let the dough rise for about 4 to 6 hours until it has doubled in size. One of the tips for sourdough bread is to fold the dough several times during the rising process.
